question.text: What is the difference between a foreign exchange swap and a currency swap?

answer.text: FX swap involves exchange of principal only, while currency swap involves exchange of both interest payments and principal
FX swap is exchange-traded while currency swap is OTC
FX swap involves only INR while currency swap involves only USD
There is no difference — they are the same product

explanation.text: **The Logic:** An FX swap involves actual exchange of two currencies on a specified date (short leg) and reverse exchange on a future date (long leg) — principal only. A currency swap (cross currency swap) involves exchange of streams of interest payments AND/OR principal amounts in different currencies. **The Trap:** Both are OTC products — the distinction is about what is exchanged (principal only vs. principal + interest streams). **Cross-Exam Context:** Swap types are tested in Series I and IV (Interest Rate Derivatives). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Currency swaps help companies convert foreign currency borrowings into domestic currency obligations, eliminating both exchange rate and foreign interest rate risk.
